Zhou Weiqing understood Duan Tianlang too well, he did not want Duan Tianlang to feel guilty about his help, and to want to stay alive, Duan Tianlang had to have something he wanted to achieve. Approaching it from the perspective of Consolidated Equipment Scrolls would definitely be the best choice.

Duan Tianlang nodded lightly, but he only smiled bitterly, “To add a piece on top of the ten pieces? That’s easy to say, but difficult to execute! It would be easier to create a ten-piece from scratch.”

Zhou Weiqing chuckled knowingly, “Junior Master, I believe, where there’s a will, there’s a way. Nothing is impossible. Look, never in my life had I practiced creating a Consolidated Equipment Scroll, but I still became a God Tier Master, did I not?”

“Huh?” This time around, not only Duan Tianlang, even Long Shiya yelped.

“You became a God Tier Master?” Duan Tianlang rushed in front of Zhou Weiqing, his hands clutching Zhou Weiqing’s shoulders. His face was filled with joy and pride, looking even more excited than his receiving decades of life extension.

chapters.title: Chapter 747
chapters.body:
Chapter 747

When the Strength Legacy had been passed on to him, his biggest regret was not being able to nurture any one of his disciples into a God Tier Master to continue passing down Strength Legacy. It could be said that if Zhou Weiqing were to become a God Tier Master, it would have a very unique meaning to Duan Tianliang.

Zhou Weiqing nodded and explained the process of him becoming a God Tier Master in simple terms.

Duan Tianliang’s temperament had always been very gentle. When he had finished hearing Zhou Weiqings explanation of the process of becoming a God Tier Master, he could not help but curse, “F*ck! This is so unfair. Do you know how much effort I’ve poured into becoming a God Tier Master that year? This is so infuriating! Big Fatty Long, the biggest regret of my life is that I couldn’t take Zhou Weiqing from your hands. At that time, I only thought that one who possessed six attributes was truly hard to come across. I myself had disciples as well, so I gave him to you. Zhou Weiqing’s consolidating talents aren’t inferior to his cultivation at all!”

Big Fatty Long laughed to the extent that his eyes could not be seen.

At this moment, noisy footsteps sounded from the outside.

‘Weiqing,” the voice had reached before the person. Next, a white shadow barged into the tent, paying no attention to the people around, and dove head first into Zhou Weiqing’s embrace. “Isn’t this Tian’er?” thought Zhou Weiqing.

It had been a year since they last met, but time had not left any traces on Tian’er, instead, she looked more attractive. However, her pretty face right then was filled with tears, like a pear flower with raindrops on it, arousing the sympathy of others.

Zhou Weiqing had left for one whole year! How could she not miss him? She clung to his waist tightly, as if was afraid of losing him.

Right after Tian’er, Shangguan Xue’er and Shangguan Fei’er also walked in. Shangguan Xue’er had her usual cold demeanor whereas Shangguan Fei’er pouted, staring at the scene of Tian’er hugging Zhou Weiqing. It was apparent that she was a little envious.

After them, Mu En, Hua Feng, Ming Yu and the other upper echelons of the Heavenly Bow Empire came. Zhou Weiqing had returned, and all of them immediately dropped their current tasks and rushed over.

Within a short span, Duan Tianliang’s tent was fully occupied with people.

Under the gazes of the crowd, Tian’er had finally loosened her hug for Zhou Weiqing, and two shades of blush appeared on her lovely face.

However, Zhou Weiqing did not give her any mercy and said something soothing by her ear with a low voice.

Tian’er’s pretty face turned even redder but still nodded at him. Zhou Weiqing’s face was instantly filled with elation and joy.

Shangguan Fei’er walked over, pulled away Zhou Weiqing, and took Tian’er into her embrace, “Watch it, you bastard. Tian’er is our key target to be protected, you’re not allowed to hug her too tightly.”

Zhou Weiqing had just asked if Tian’er was pregnant. After obtaining her affirmation, knowing that he would be a father soon, it would be weird for him to be happy.

Ming Yu glared at Zhou Weiqing menacingly, and said, “You know how to find your way back, don’t you?!”

Zhou Weiqing laughed out loud, stepped forward, and gave him a really big hug.

Helplessness could be seen on Ming Yu’s face. He gave him a light punch of his fist on the back and smiles finally appeared on his face. No matter what, it was good to have Zhou Weiqing back. He was the spiritual pillar of the entire Heavenly Bow Empire! The upper echelons of the Heavenly Bow Empire were almost built up by himself. If he did not return, although there no problems with Ming Yu commanding the army, but there were far more other matters that were not under his control. Everything was different without the name of Zhou Weiqing!

After he had hugged Ming Yu, Zhou Weiqing turned toward the Shangguan sisters, gave them each a hug, and then proceeded to give a bear hug to each person in the tent without missing anyone out.

When we had walked to a person hiding in the corners, he was stunned momentarily, but still speared his arms in the same manner, “Sister Ruse, you’ve worked hard.”

This person was Xiao Ruse, but she did not look well and seemed to be a little pale. Nevertheless, she still stepped towards Zhou Weiqing’s embrace.

Zhou Weiqing heard her mumble faintly, “I’ve yet to exist when you were born, and you were old when I was born.” Those were ten simple words, but it made Zhou Weiqing’s heart shudder violently. Although he knew that he should not get himself into too many romantic affairs, he could not help but feel great pain now. Numerous memories of him following Xiao Ruse flashed in his heart.

Although it was over in an instant, Zhou Weiqing still felt that his initially high spirits have plummeted. Hugging Xiao Ruse hard, he behaved out of her expectations by saying in a low voice, “Time is never a problem, age is never a limit.”

Xiao Ruse’s eyes shone but dimmed quickly; she retreated from his embrace to the side.

Xiao Ruse indeed was much older than Zhou Weiqing, but she was now past her thirties. On the other hand, Zhou Weiqing was only in his twenties. The battles and adversities all those years resulted in her no longer being the beauty of the same rank as the Shangguan Triplets and Tian’er. It was no wonder that she was ashamed of herself.

As the tent was so crowded at that moment, it was only natural that Zhou Weiqing did not pursue her. He turned his body to face the people, and gave a deep 90-degree bow, “Everyone, you’ve worked hard. I thank you from the bottom of my heart.”

It was such a simple sentence with only a few words, but it reddened the eyes of the people.

Ming Yu’s feelings struck deep. He signed internally, ‘The one with the talent to become an Emperor! This guy was definitely made to become an Emperor!’. Although he thought of himself to be greatly talented, he had not been able to become the General. Zhou Weiqing may not be his match in terms of talent, but his charisma was far greater than that of his. Thus, he was able to garner the support of so many people.

Zhou Weiqing may seem to not have done much in the process of the Heavenly Bow Empire’s fall until today. All the things he had done were the really important matters — he was the soul of the Heavenly Bow Empire!

Ming Yu coughed and said, “Let’s continue our chat somewhere else, so as not to interrupt Master.”

Duan Tianliang chuckled, and said, “I don’t mind the interruption. It’s just that my place here is quite small. Weiqing won’t be able to get close to anyone, hehe.”

The crowd could not help but laugh.

Exiting Duan Tianliang’s room, the crowd arrived at the big tent of main military force. Ming Yu had Zhou Weiqing sit on the primary seat, but Zhou Weiqing objected and signaled for Ming Yu to have the seat instead. Eventually, the primary seat remained vacant, and there were two more chairs at the bottom.

After a brief greeting session, it arrived the discussion of important matters. Zhou Weiqing naturally did not have to tell everyone his experiences. Summarized by a few sentences, the discussion quickly moved to the current situation of the Heavenly Bow Empire.

Ming Yu’s explanation was far more detailed than Kou Rui’s, and it included his own opinions and interpretations. According to Ming Yu’s words, the Heavenly Bow Empire may appear to be in a good standing at the moment, it was in a dangerous situation in reality. There was no problem with the latter, but the concern was, for an army of hundreds of thousands to face the Bai Da Empire behind the Kalise Empire, could they really withstand it? At this point, Ming Yu had no confidence as well. They could only give their best in everything they were about to do until the very end.

The most pressing issue was the fight which could break out anytime soon. Heavenly Bow Empire must take an offensive stance, especially if it were to rescue the royalties of Heavenly Bow Empire and Zhou Weiqing’s family. However, this was the biggest challenge at the moment. A forceful invasion would be impossible, even with Zhou Weiqing’s family out of the picture, no warrior of Heavenly Bow Empire would be willing to risk the annihilation of the empire’s royalties and attack the Heavenly Bow Empire. If the order were to be implemented by force, morale would be receiving a strong blow.

“…, Weiqing, you must make a decision as soon as possible,” emphasized Ming Yu, after explaining their current situation.

Zhou Weiqing nodded lightly, and inquired, “How’s the enemies’ military assembly near the Heavenly Bow City?”

Upon his questioning, Ming Yu’s face turned sour, “Those bastards from the Kalise and Bai Da Empire… the tactic they are utilizing is to strengthen their defenses to the point that it’s close to invulnerable as Heavenly Bow City itself isn’t too huge and isn’t able to fit too many troops. They’re targeting Heavenly Bow City as the center, clearing the Star Forest surrounding Heavenly Bow City, and have set their base at Heavenly Bow City as the center. A large number of Star Trees have been cut down and transported away. Their current military assembly consists of approximately fifteen regiments, but their real force could never match ours. These fifteen regiments include the ten regiments which the Kalise Empire had been gathering for two years, while the rest were sent from Bai Da Empire. As of now, they show no signs of reinforcing their troops. It’s obvious that their current purpose is to hold us back here, and will await the Dandun and Bai Da Empire to make a move before we strike. ”

Zhou Weiqing squinted his eyes but did not voice out his opinions. His few close friends who knew him best could tell from his dark demeanor that Little Fatty was really enraged.

To have his home being invaded was a great shame to Zhou Weiqing. These invaders had even destroyed his homeland, destroyed the Star Forest, and used his family to threaten the troops from advancing. One could imagine how furious Zhou Weiqing could be.

There were countless people in the meeting hall, but it has turned to a pin-drop silence. Everyone’s gaze fell on Zhou Weiqing, waiting for his decision.

Zhou Weiqing had been silent for five minutes until he lifted his head, eyes shining, “Admiral Ming Yu, if we were to launch our attacks, how much time do you need for the preparations?”

Ming Yu answered without hesitation, “All is ready. We can launch our offense any time.”

“Alright,” Zhou Weiqing exclaimed. He stood up abruptly, with rage in his eyes, and all of a sudden, everyone in the meeting hall could sense the strong aura radiating from him.

“Since things are what it is now, I’ll give you one last day for the preparations. Tomorrow night is the time we’ll act, as for the actual operation plans, we’ll discuss now.”

Admiration flowed from Ming Yu’s eyes. As indecisiveness would lead to trouble, for Zhou Weiqing to be able to make a firm decision within such a short duration was an essential aspect that a successful, high-ranking individual ought to have.

Ming Yu excused the officers for the time being. Only several people of the core ranks were left, and with Zhou Weiqing, they discussed the detailed plans of the battle.

Just like what Ming Yu himself had said, to take back Heavenly Bow City and to revive Heavenly Bow Empire, he had been preparing for a few months’ time, they could move out any time. The attack methods of each aspect had long been finalized.

The group had discussed for the whole four hours until they ended the high-ranking meeting.

Shangguan Bing’er was pulled away by Shangguan Xue’er and Shangguan Fei’er, Zhou Weiqing was left with Tian’er by them, from Bing’er, they could similarly know what had happened to Zhou Weiqing. Furthermore, before they had departed for Mysterious Heavenly Mainland, Tian’er had given Zhou Weiqing to Bing’er. No matter what, they would have to repay her kindness. They all would be living together for their whole life, therefore, it was beneficial to all to improve their relationships.

Tian’er pulled Zhou Weiqing’s hand and led him back to where they stayed. She could clearly feel Zhou Weiqing’s gloominess at the time being.

‘Little Fatty, we can definitely succeed,” said Tian’er after she closed the door, and immediately threw herself into Zhou Weiqing’s embrace.

Zhou Weiqing hugged her gently as if he was afraid of hurting her, and caressed her long hair, “I’m fine. I too believe that we’ll succeed. Tian’er, will you promise me one thing?”

Tian’er shook her head, “If you’re going to forbid me from joining the battle tomorrow, you can stop. I must join. If not, how am I going to face your parents in the future?”

Zhou Weiqing smiled bitterly, “Tian’er, you’re wrong, if I happen to let my father know that you’ve gone to fight while pregnant, he’ll never forgive me! Listen to me, my dear Tian’er, please don’t be involved in this battle. Are you still worried if I have Master with me?”

Tian’er pouted and complained in his embrace, “No, I must go. Xue’er, Fei’er and Bing’er are all so amazing; they all want to rescue your parents with you. If I don’t go, what will your parents think of me? Don’t you know that the first impression is really important? Dear husband, just let me go with you. Please, I beg of you, even my father says that I’ll be fine.”

While Tian’er had called him dear husband, half of Zhou Weiqing’s body had turned numb. If he was not worried about the possibilities of his family’s life being threatened by the Heavenly King-tier powerhouses in Bai Da Empire, he might very well be making love to Tian’er right there and then.

“Tian’er, what did father-in-law say when you were back in the Heavenly Snow Mountain? Zhou Weiqing had not been able to head to the Heavenly Snow Mountain with Tian’er, but he was concerned about Xue Ao’tian’s opinions. Never mind the other matters, Tian’er had been bearing his child when they had yet to be married – this was no small matter! Tian’er was the only daughter of Xue Ao’tian, the Mountain Lord of the Heavenly Snow Mountain. However, judging from the fact that Xue Ao’tian had allowed Tian’er to come back, he was not really angry.

Upon hearing her father being mentioned, Tian’er stuck her tongue out, and said, “Father had been quite furious. I was close to not being able to return; it was my first time seeing anger and helplessness plastered on father’s face at the same time. However, he could not stand my constant coaxing and pestering, and hence, I’m back. However, father mentioned that if you’re not going to see him after you’ve returned to the mainland, he will come and meet you himself.”

Zhou Weiqing’s facial muscles twitched. He could perfectly imagine the expressions of Xue Ao’tian clenching his teeth while he was uttering these words.

“Regarding this, my dear, as you know, I really can’t leave. After I’ve rescued my parents, I’ll pay father-in-law another visit for sure, and to fix our dates as well.”

Tian’er said softly, “I know. You have important things to attend to, even if father really came, he can’t do anything to you either. After all, we already have our child.” While saying, she caressed her abdomen which was still flat at the moment, radiating with happiness.

Zhou Weiqing immediately asked, “What did father-in-law say about our child? When will the baby be born?”

Looking at Zhou Weiqing’s expressions of anticipation, Tian’er chuckled and said, “It’s still early; at least another three years. I’ve recalled correctly, giving birth to a baby will take a long time due to our Divine Heavenly Spirit Tiger Bloodline. On top of the protection of Essence Bloodline and Saint Energy now, father said that our child’s defensive abilities will be far stronger than ours. My stomach is invulnerable.”

“Umm…, things like this exist?” Zhou Weiqing stared at her, stupefied.

Tian’er said proudly, “Of course, it’s only natural for us to have our own means of survival since the inheritance of our Divine Heavenly Spirit Tiger Bloodline ages ago. Once the females of our Divine Heavenly Spirit Tiger Bloodline are impregnated, the bloodline will transform into a unique energy and fuse with the child into a single entity, effectively protecting him or her. In addition, I have Saint Energy. Father said that under the nurture of our bloodline powers and the protection of Saint Energy, the child’s defense is so strong to the extent that he or she can’t be harmed even by my father’s attacks. What else is there for you to be concerned? I must go tomorrow. Don’t you forget, I’m the mother of the child. Will my love for our child be any less than yours? Without any assurance from my father, I’ll never raise such demands.”

Zhou Weiqing stared at the stubbornness and persistence in Tian’er’s eyes, and said in defeat, “Alright, but without my permission, you must never attack haphazardly, okay?”

“Okay!” said Tian’er in giggles and joy, seeing that he had agreed.

Zhou Weiqing said, “Regarding the matters of the Nether Realm, what did father-in-law have to say?”

The smile on Tian’er’s face disappeared a little, and said,”Father is also worried about this. The reason why he wants you to meet him is also to talk about this issue other than our matters. Father did not say much about the situation in Nether Realm; he just asked me to share with you that my mother had not been by our side because she had to guard the entrance of the Nether Realm. As for my mother’s cultivation, it’s nothing more than a vassal of the true guardians.”

Upon registering Tian’er’s words, Zhou Weiqing’s facial expression changed. He knew very well that the Entropic Nether Tiger Phelia was a Heavenly God-tier powerhouse, and compared to the Mountain Lord of Heavenly Snow Mountain, Xue Ao’tian, she was only slightly weaker. For a Heavenly God-tier powerhouse like her to be a mere vassal, how much stronger would the true guardians of the Nether Realm’s entrance be? His guess was the pinnacle of Heavenly God-tier.

Under such tight security of the group of powerhouses, it had indirectly testified the existence and horrors of the Nether Realm. It seemed that what the family of huge dragons had encountered, as he had expected, was the Nether Realm’s conspiracy. In addition, it was highly possible for the Nether Realm to be associated with some of the powers on the mainland. Judging from the current situation, all the traces had been pointing to the Blood Red Hell. The Destruction Attribute they had demonstrated in the Heavenly Jewel Tournament was obvious to all. Furthermore, the sudden aggression of Dandun Empire displayed their ambitions. The only thing was this – was everything really this simple?

If it was only the Dandun and Bai Da Empire that’s involved in a conspiracy under the support of the Blood Red Hell, then would the Zhongtian Empire, Heaven’s Expanse Palace, Wan Shou Empire, and Heavenly Snow Mountain remain seated at the sidelines? Zhou Weiqing had known all along that there was never such a thing as permanent enemies in this world — there were only permanent benefits. Once the existence of the Blood Red Hell threatened the survival of the entire mainland, it was not impossible for contact between Heaven’s Expanse Palace and Heavenly Snow Mountain to be established!

The truth would never show itself that easily. The Blood Red Hell alone would not prompt anyone to make a move on a whim. However, if there were to be contact between the Nether Realm and the Boundless Mainland, there might be even more situations. And all of these would only be apparent due to the sudden movements of the Blood Red Hell and the Dandun Empire, but things have just barely started.

It seemed that no matter if it was for Tian’er or the mainland’s sake, it was necessary for him to pay another visit to Heavenly Snow Mountain, at the very least. The war waged by the Wan Shou Empire on the Zhong Tian Empire must be halted, and things must be clarified before any further action was taken.

After organizing his thoughts, Zhou Weiqing did not accompany Tian’er any longer. He had just returned and there was another major operation the next night, so there were many things which had to be dealt with.

Throughout the rest of the day, Zhou Weiqing barely had any rest. He had been meeting up with the upper echelons of the Heavenly Bow Empire without a break, and have taken care of the preparations for the battle tomorrow night with Ming Yu. At the same time, their plans were confidential and their enemies must never get hold of the information.

Regarding Zhou Weiqing’s influence since his return, Ming Yu had the deepest thoughts. Everybody in the upper echelons, regardless if they were re-established from the disbanded army of the Heavenly Bow Empire or not, from the Peerless Regiment or if they were gathered by Zhou Weiqing, each and every one of them had a one-to-one meeting with Zhou Weiqing.

Of course, news of the attack was only known by the few people of the highest ranks, Zhou Weiqing had only told these officers that they would be launching their final round of offense in the near future. At the same time, he had also guaranteed that the royalty of the Heavenly Bow Empire would be rescued during the attacks.

The time he had spent meeting each person was different. Nevertheless, Ming Yu noticed that every officer who had been consoled by Zhou Weiqing would show absolute trust toward him. For most of the officers, although they greatly admired Ming Yu, he was still considered an outsider. On the other hand, Zhou Weiqing was different; his position in the Heavenly Bow Empire had always been essential. The godson of the Emperor, and the relative of the Admiral. On top of that, everything that the Heavenly Bow Empire had achieved to this date could be considered as the contributions of Zhou Weiqing. His parents were trapped in Heavenly Bow City, and he would never take his parent’s lives as a joke. Therefore, these men naturally trusted him completely.

Within half a day’s time, Zhou Weiqing had completely resolved the issues of the army’s morale. As for the rest of the military tasks, they were naturally assigned to the officers.

After the most pressing issue had been resolved, Zhou Weiqing brought the Shangguan Triplets and Tian’er back to his room and quarantined them temporarily after lunch the next day. At the same time, they were also waiting for the last moment. On the other hand, Ming Yu had become the busiest, relaying countless military orders from the tent of the Heavenly Bow Commander.

Ming Yu realized that he was starting to grow fonder of his partner, Zhou Weiqing. Yes, he had always seen Zhou Weiqing as his collaborator and partner who fought together, while the Heavenly Bow Empire was his platform to demonstrate his talents.

Although Zhou Weiqing rarely appeared and seemed irresponsible, Ming Yu realized that he could not be without him. Zhou Weiqing’s long-term vision and charisma, combined with his abilities in military affairs, were the key to the rise of the Heavenly Bow Empire. And what satisfied Ming Yu the most was that Zhou Weiqing would never comment on how he commanded the military, as he trusted him completely.

The most important battle of the Heavenly Bow Empire since its revival was about to begin, no explanations were needed for the importance of this battle. Both Zhou Weiqing or Ming Yu would not allow failure.

The skies gradually darkened, and the Heavenly Bow Empire was showered in a faint layer of red under the afterglow illumination.

Heavenly Bow City had been one of the most beautiful cities of the mainland. It was also the only city which stood amidst the woods, but now, it seemed a little defeated.

Around Heavenly Bow City, large areas of Star Trees had been cut down and transported to the Kalise and Bai Da Empire, resulting in a vast piece of empty land around the city. At the very least, looking outward from within the city, there were no covers of any sorts within ten miles. This had effectively prevented assaults.

The inside of Heavenly Bow City seemed depressed; the northern gates and the cities were all filled with armies. The city gate had long been shut. The population in the city was not even one-third of what it was during the ruling era of the Heavenly Bow Empire as those who were capable of escaping had all escaped. Especially after the armies who had recovered the lands while reviving the Heavenly Bow Empire, many citizens of Heavenly Bow City had chosen to escape, making their way to the new areas which the Heavenly Bow Empire had just seized.

Facing the heavy outflow of citizens, Kalise Empire had no choice but to close the gate, not allowing any citizens of Heavenly Bow City to leave so easily. Even with that, the people in Heavenly Bow City now stayed indoors, resulting in an empty and lonely city. It could only be hoped that all the civilians of the Heavenly Bow Empire were secretly anticipating the recovery of the armies of Heavenly Bow outside the city.

The palace of the Heavenly Bow Empire seemed lonely under the afterglow. The main hall of the palace, concurrently the biggest hall which contained the Heavenly Bow Empire’s main hall, was engulfed by absolute darkness.

In the darkness, there was a faint layer of red blood light. It was only during the times with the strongest sunlight that one could see the looming outlines of the palace.

Right in front of this palace, there stood three people, all donned in Chinese costumes.

Among the three, the one standing in the center was an old man, donning a long black golden silk gown with dragon patterns, exuding a cold demeanor, while his eyes were full of arrogance.

The two other elderly people at his side looked older. However, they slightly resembled each other, so it was obvious that they were brothers. Both of them wore fire-red long gowns, but their manners toward the elderly in a black gown in the center were very respectful.

The old man in a black gown asked flatly, “How are the arrangements?”

The red-gowned old man to his left said in respect, “Reporting to Heavenly Emperor, everything has been arranged. The news we’ve released was that only a Darkness Heavenly King had come, if those people from the Heavenly Bow Empire dared to touch us, they won’t be going anywhere.”

The red-gowned old man to his right chuckled, and said, “Heavenly Emperor, rest assured, I really don’t understand how can we bother you to make your appearance. Although the military standards of the Heavenly Bow Empire aren’t bad, they don’t really have any exceptional abilities, they’re not to be part of our concerns. We’ve bothered six Heavenly Kings, and you, a Heavenly Emperor to personally sit in – it’s overkill.”

The old man in the black gown said, “You can’t be careless too. At this frontline, we only have these armies for now, while the bigger armies have all been assigned to the southern front. For the overall plans of the Empire, we can’t allow any problems here. Otherwise, though those in the Heavenly Bow Empire’s armies may not be large in number, they can focus their men in a single area. Once the Kalise Empire has been wiped out, and the Bai Da Empire takes fire, the whole battle will be affected. On our side, we’ll just have to defend. What other dissatisfactions do you have with such a simple mission?”

“None, none at all.” The two old man quickly bowed and said.

The black-gowned old man turned his body and looked at the huge palace behind him and slightly furrowed his brows, “A mere tiny Ninth Jewel like this dares to activate a skill like the Fate Denying Seal? It’ll be problematic even for me to attempt to release the seal. On the other hand, this seal is also beneficial as it’s a way to hold back the Heavenly Bow Empire. Otherwise, before we’ve secured most of the control of the mainland, we still can’t make our moves as we please against the normal armies.”

This man in a black gown was Meng Gui or Heavenly Emperor Meng Gui as people called him. He used to be an independent cultivator, the same as Long Shiya, one of the Six Ultimate Heavenly Emperors. The only difference was that Long Shiya was more active in the north while the Heavenly Emperor Meng Gui hustled in the south. His Darkness Energy had been trained to excellence, and he was a famed figure in the world of independent cultivation as well. However, nobody knew when he had joined Dandun Empire.

The two old figures in red gown were indeed brothers, who came from Blood Red Hell. Named Huo Li and Huo Mei respectively, they were strong opponents nurtured by Blood Red Hell.

Other than them, there was each a Heavenly King-tier powerhouse on guard in the four directions around the huge palace of the Heavenly Bow Empire. It be could be said that the defense right here was as solid as metal walls and moats of boiling water.

According to their investigations, there was at least another Heavenly King-tier powerhouse residing in the Heavenly Bow Empire, and that person was God Tier Master Duan Tianliang. The assassin had actually been sent by Blood Red Hell to kill Zhou Weiqing so that the Heavenly Bow Empire would fall on its own. By then, their defense at the western front would no longer mean anything. Although the Fei Li Empire was a big country, there were the constraints by the Wan Shou Empire in the northern regions. As long as they did not launch their attack first, the Fei Li Empire would not dare to advance as well. Furthermore, on the Heavenly Bow Empire’s side, they possessed more than two hundred thousand military forces during their peak. Even if Fei Li Empire made any moves, they would not result in anything much.

However, who would have imagined for the assassination to fail? After Ming Yu had led the Heavenly Bow Empire’s armies to grow stronger, it resulted in the sharp decline in army forces on top of the exhaustion by the battle in Xuan Yue City. Their overall powers had even fallen to the extent that they might be no match for the Heavenly Bow Empire now.

Of course, to these few powerhouses above Heavenly King Tier, they did not bother much. If it were not for the limitations imposed by the Mainland Heavenly Jewel Master Agreement, with their combined forces, they had no reason to be afraid of the Heavenly Bow Empire. As to why they were stationed here, one of the factors was that they had taken the whole situation into account. Another being that they did not wish to surprise the Heaven’s Expanse Palace and the faraway Heavenly Snow Mountain. It was still not the right time for them to expose all their cards.

At this moment, the old man in a black gown who had been staring at the palace engulfed by the Fate Denying Seal raised his head all of a sudden and looked toward the sky. Two rays of cold light shot from his eyes like lighting. He shrieked, “Get your ass out here.”

“The word ‘ass’, do you have the rights to say that to me?” A voice sounded from the air in disdain. Within an instant, the space above the palace lit up, and six dazzling colors exploded like fireworks and descended from the sky, enveloping the Fate Denying Seal within it.

Midair, a big fat person stepped out, and arrived above the palace, both his arms gesturing to pressure the rest, and shrieked, “Get all of your asses out here.”

The six colors suddenly expanded and simultaneously pressed toward each direction, but the strange thing was that these light rays seemed to have eyes. The buildings were not damaged at all.

Four silhouettes shot out like four streaks of lightning toward four different directions, with the addition of the two brothers, Huo Li and Huo Mei, leaping onto the sky, the six of them surrounded the big fat person in the air.

Meng Gui’s iris contracted instantly, “Six Ultimate Heavenly Emperor Long Shiya. Big Fatty Long, you dare to stand against the Blood Red Hell?”

The big fat person who had appeared out of nowhere was Long Shiya. Upon hearing Meng Gui’s words, Long Shiya said with disdain, “What about the Blood Red Hell? Who do I fear? I’ve even made frequent visits to that fellow Xue Ao’tian in Heavenly Snow Mountain. Meng Gui, you’re also an independent cultivator with Heavenly Emperor-tier cultivation. To be someone’s lackey at your old age, aren’t you ashamed?”

Meng Gui took one step forward. The next instant, he had come to an approximate of twenty meters in front of Long Shiya in the air, standing opposite him in a confrontational manner.

Looking at the eleven circling Heavenly Jewels beneath and above Long Shiya, he continuously released his own aura to withstand the pressure flowing from Long Shiya.

“Big Fatty Long, others may fear you, but I’m not bothered. That Six Ultimate Heavenly Path Formation of yours may be fascinating, but on your own, you won’t be faring well here. What about your old partner Duan Tianliang? Why don’t you call him out?”

Upon Meng Gui’s appearance in the air, the surrounding six Heavenly King-tier powerhouses felt a sharp decrease of pressure, and secretly let out a breath of relief. Long Shiya had been addressed as The Mightiest below the Heavenly Gods, and that name was not without reason.

Long Shiya said scornfully, “Why would I need the additional appearance of old Duan just to deal with an old man like you and the rest of you? You think too highly of yourself.”

Meng Gui grunted but showed no changes in his facial expression. “There’s no meaning for you to debate with me here. It’s been some time since I’ve dealt with your Six Ultimate Heavenly Path Formation. We shall see today if you’ve improved in these years.”

While saying that, his side of the sky darkened abruptly, and forcefully pushed back part of the six colors of light rays which Long Shiya had released in the air. The absolute darkness was mixed with a concentrated aura of insanity.

“Eh? So I was right. I’ve been thinking how the Blood Red Hell have been engaging you; it’s this damned Destruction Attribute.” Long Shiya was not really surprised. With both his arms folded behind him, his demeanor remained the same even if he was surrounded by that many powerhouses.

chapters.title: Chapter 748
chapters.body:
Chapter 748

Meng Gui said flatly, “Don’t be envious of the things you can’t get your hands on. Big Fatty Long, the Destruction Attribute is one that surpasses Saint Attribute. That Six Ultimate Heavenly Path Formation of yours may be fascinating, but I’m afraid it’ll be left here today.”

Just as his words fell, six rays of Destruction Energy, filled with insanity and a hysterical aura, almost rose from six directions simultaneously. The concentrated Destruction aura instantly compressed the six colors of Six Ultimate Heavenly Path Formation released by Long Shiya inward. On top of Meng Gui’s direct confrontation, Long Shiya had been pressured into a disadvantageous situation within an instant.

Long Shiya’s facial expression changed immediately. Although he had heard of the Destruction Attribute from Zhou Weiqing, never had he expected for this Destruction Attribute to be so otherworldly. We would not have taken these people seriously if they did not possess the Destruction Attribute, but the situation right then was obviously different.

A cold grin appeared on Meng Gui’s face, “I guess you’ve expected this, Big Fatty Long. The Destruction Attribute isn’t only powerful, its powers will also grow stronger the more its users are. If we’re just using our own attributes, there’s no way for us to hold you back. However, I now have similarities with them due to our possession of Destruction Attribute. Under the effects of the Destruction Attribute, you can only dream of leaving here. I’ve long heard that you’ve accepted a disciple; this battle today is prepared especially for you. Today, I shall not let you go anywhere.”

Under the pressure of the Destructive Attribute which was being released at the same time by a Heavenly Emperor and six Heavenly King-tier powerhouses, Long Shiya’s Six Ultimate Heavenly path Formation had been compressed to an area of merely several meters around his body, and this area was still shrinking. Once the Six Ultimate Heavenly Path Formation was no longer capable of protecting him, the blows he were to receive would be a deadly one from these seven mighty opponents.

However, what puzzled the Heavenly Emperor Meng Gui was that Long Shiya could still laugh at a time like this, and he was laughing in disdain at that laughed in disdain.

Out of nowhere, a shadow as black as ink appeared at the back of a Heavenly King-tier powerhouse on the left.

The Heavenly King from Blood Red Hell kept on activating his Destruction Attribute and did not notice the shadow behind him at all.

Even a Heavenly Emperor-tier powerhouse like Heavenly Emperor Meng Gui had not noticed how the person appeared.

Heavy, strong fists easily gripped onto the Blood Red Hell Heavenly King’s neck. Everything was too sudden for the Heavenly King. Due to his cultivation base that was at that of a Heavenly King-tier, the Heavenly Energy within him seemed to have exploded in an instant from the ambush.

Furthermore, the Blood Red Hell Heavenly King was also extremely cruel. He had chosen to detonate his Heavenly Core Nucleus without any hesitation.

To have his neck grabbed by someone without any warning, what did it mean? It meant that the individual’s cultivation was far superior to his. Only by detonating his Heavenly Nucleus Core and activating his strongest attack to frighten his enemies would he have a chance to stay alive. Once one had reached the rank of a Heavenly King, one’s wisdom would escalate along with the development of the cultivation base. Without any hesitation, he had made the right choice.

Unfortunately, while his decision was correct, the reality was different from what he wished for.

The terrifying powers of devour made the Blood Red Hell Heavenly King instantly feel as if his body was caught in a whirlpool in the depths of the ocean. His will to ignite the Heavenly Nucleus Core had momentarily frozen. Then, his Heavenly Energy began to flow out of his body, whereas his Heavenly Nucleus Core was no longer under his control. It was absorbed by an attraction force out of nowhere to the extent that it was stuck.

With the sudden decrease in the Destruction Attribute’s Heavenly Energy’s suppression, Long Shiya’s lights of six colors brightened substantially. It was only when the explosion had brought along huge energy waves, only did the group from Blood Red Hell notice that something was amiss.

In unison, they directed their gaze to the heavenly King-tier powerhouse who had been ambushed. However, when they had come to their senses and wanted to go to his rescue, it was too late.

Blocked by the Heavenly King-tier powerhouse’s body, there was no way for them to see what had struck the Heavenly King-tier powerhouse. What they had seen was only his body flying backward at top speed, moving far away.

At this moment, the Blood Red Hell powerhouses did not dare to make a move. The famed Six Ultimate Heavenly Emperor Long Shiya was so powerful that only their great number managed to suppress him. One must know that even for Meng Gui, his cultivation level only laid in the middle ranks of Heavenly Emperor Tier. If he were to have a one-on-one confrontation with Six Ultimate Heavenly Emperor Long Shiya, he would not be able to stand for thirty minutes.

The Six Ultimate Heavenly Emperor had been known for his great endurance in battle. Ordinary Heavenly Jewel Masters could not easily comprehend Heavenly Jewels with six attributes – his name of The Mightiest below Heavenly God was clearly given for a reason. Even three or four Heavenly Emperor-tier powerhouses would be no match for him and the reason he could be restricted was all because of the Destruction Attribute.

“Everyone, stay cautious!” Meng Gui shouted, his demeanor no longer as relaxed as before. At the same time, hesitation arose in his heart.

The Heavenly King-tier powerhouse who had been flown away could no longer be seen, falling into Heavenly Bow City and vanished.

Meng Gui was contemplating if he should strike Long Shiya now, instead of being at a standstill. The reason he chose the current method of suppression was that it was the sole method that would minimize their casualties. Otherwise, upon Long Shiya’s retaliation, they would be paying a deadly price – especially him – as he would definitely be Long Shiya’s first target.

By holding Long Shiya down with the Destruction Attribute, he would be further suppressed by their aura if he were to make the first move, as long as they did not strike. Under repeated attacks like these, Long Shiya would no longer be able to stand not long after.

However, who would have expected such abrupt changes to the situation? One of the six surrounding Heavenly King-tier powerhouses had vanished all of a sudden, resulting in the decline of their pressure on Long Shiya, effectively lengthening the time that Six Ultimate Heavenly Emperor could hold himself. Of course, it would bear the same outcome if they were to continue their suppression. However, nobody knew how strong their hidden enemy was.

No matter how much Meng Gui thought of it, he could not wrap his head around the fact that such a powerhouse still existed in Heavenly Bow Empire. Duan Tianliang was powerful, but he was only at the Heavenly King Tier and it was impossible for him to capture a powerhouse of the same level without leaving a trace! To sneak behind a Heavenly King-tier powerhouse without giving himself away brought no guarantee that the Heavenly Emperor would be capable of such a feat.

A cynical expression surfaced on Long Shiya’s face; his previous nervousness and shock were actually being feigned. Indeed, the powers of the enemies before him were not weak, but he had stepped into their trap on purpose. Without the strong attraction force, it would be more taxing for him to get rid of these enemies, but now… would they dare to loosen their suppression on him?

Right at this moment, another heavenly King-tier powerhouse in the other direction similarly let out a grunt, and his body flew backward immediately.

At this point, Meng Gui’s demeanor finally changed, before commanding quickly without any hesitation, “Attack!”

The strong Destructive Attribute instantly transformed from suppression to physical attacks. Greyish brown light mixed with intense flames exploded from the four heavenly King-tier powerhouses simultaneously. At the same time, Consolidated Radiance shone from each of their bodies, and they demonstrated their full power.

Meng Gui had made his judgment at that instant. If there was a possibility that the first Heavenly King-tier powerhouse had been ambushed due to his being off-guard, it would not be as simple as mere carelessness for a second Heavenly King-tier powerhouse to be struck in the same manner. The hidden opponent must be extremely powerful. If the situation were to remain the same, although they triumphed in numbers, it would be hard to determine the outcome of the day’s battle when they had their backs exposed to their enemy. The best course of action would only be by eliminating Long Shiya before searching for the concealed opponent.

When the four Heavenly Kings of Blood Red Hell launched their attacks together, Meng Gui appeared before Long Shiya in a flash. Amidst the dark illumination, each of his hands was covered with a Consolidated Equipment, of two-feet-long Ghostclaw Spikes.

All five of his fingers had the Ghostclaw Spikes, specializing in breaking the defense of Heavenly Energy. In addition to the insane Destruction Attribute, his rush forward had split the air in an instant, forming a long black hole. Without a doubt, he had also exerted his mightiest offense.

Under the pressure of Destruction Attribute, the powers of their combined attack would be the greatest. As long as this attack could deliver a fatal blow to Long Shiya, the rest of the battle would be much easier. Meng Gui even had the confidence of annihilating Long Shiya by himself. Till then, the four Heavenly Kings would guard the perimeters against the enemy lurking in the dark. Once they had gotten rid of Long Shiya, they would deal with the enemy and emerge victoriously.

However, the unfolding of events had always differed from one’s predictions.

The stacked powers of the simultaneous onslaught of the Heavenly Emperor and four Heavenly Kings with the energies of the same attribute may be extremely powerful, but would they really be able to harm Long Shiya? The next moment, Six Ultimate Heavenly Emperor gave his answer.

Upon the actions of his opponents, Long Shiya also moved, gesturing both of his arms to draw circles by his sides. The six colored light rays first shrank by two meters and then exploded in an instant. This time, as the six colors exploded, six silhouettes appeared around Long Shiya.

The silhouettes all looked like him – red for the Fire Attribute, green for the Wind Attribute, blue for the Water Attribute, yellow for the Earth Attribute, gold for the Light Attribute and black for the Blackness Attribute.

When the six different colored Long Shiya’s appeared, they acted the same as well. Almost instantly, the Blood Red Hell powerhouses seemed to be facing six of the strongest Heavenly Emperors.

Along with a deafening sound, the five shadows were flung away. Long Shiya had actually knocked away the five Blood Red Hell powerhouses all by himself. On the other hand, he still casually remained at his original position; the only ones who had vanished were the six silhouettes he had released.

Pouting, Long Shiya did not continue the pursuit, “What Destruction Attribute? It’s nothing much.”

“Impossible,” while being hurled away, Meng Gui exclaimed in shock. Among the six shadows released by Long Shiya, two that were summoned were of the Light and Water Attribute respectively. The powerful hit he suffered did not differ much from Long Shiya himself and seemed like a full-blown attack. Even with the heightened powers of the Destructive Attribute, he had been flung away, and the blood in his body was surging. What was even more incomprehensible to Meng Gui was that Long Shiya’s strike had a unique aura which had actually dispersed his Destructive Attribute, nullifying its effects.

Long Shiya laughed aloud, and said, “Little Meng Gui, nothing’s impossible in this world. It’s just your short-sighted vision. What is Destructive Attribute in the face of the Saint Attribute? Everything is useless.”

For him to be able to withstand the hit, it was not entirely due to his own powers. It was true that Long Shiya’s cultivation was strong. Furthermore, he had the combination of six attributes. He had no reasons to be wary of the Destructive Attribute as the four Saint Attributes would not even amount to much suppression when he fused the six Elemental Attributes. Other than that, Zhou Weiqing had injected the real Saint Energy Galaxia into his body. Supported by the Saint Energy Galaxia, one could only imagine the powers that exploded from Long Shiya’s strike. Furthermore, in front of Saint Energy, how was it possible for the Destructive Attribute demonstrate its full potential?

With that, Long Shiya had solved his situation of being besieged in the midst of intersecting light rays.

Meng Gui’s plans were not that bad but compared to the young and cunning Zhou Weiqing, there was still a difference. After all, Zhou Weiqing had played a part in many conspiracies.

All of these were within Zhou Weiqing’s calculations. Meng Gui would never have expected for Zhou Weiqing to be here two hours ago. He had conducted a detailed investigation regarding the location, and with the Starcrux Saint Nucleus Core’s effects in place, even the Heavenly Emperors could not detect Zhou Weiqing’s presence.

After identifying the enemies, how could Zhou Weiqing leave them any chance?

Just as Meng Gui was crying out in shock, yet another of the four Heavenly Kings who were being bombarded away had disappeared.

The situation was just too strange and caused the expressions of the other three Blood Red Hell Heavenly Kings to change. They did not consider launching another round of attacks at Long Shiya but instead, gathered together in the span of a few breaths. They stood with their backs facing each other and surveyed their surroundings in vigilance.

Long Shiya stood midair, clasping his hands behind him, his eyes beaming with pride. He was not proud of himself, but of his beloved disciple.

To take care of three Heavenly Kings within such a short span of time without leaving a single trace. There was no doubt that Zhou Weiqing could do it!

Although Zhou Weiqing only had the cultivation base of someone at the Heavenly King Tier, with the Starcrux Saint Nucleus Core and his magnificent escalation abilities, he could definitely hold a candle against a Heavenly God-tier powerhouse. He may have pulled some tricks in his victorious battle against his father-in-law at the Heaven’s Expanse Palace on Heavenly Jewel Island, but much of the events were due to his efforts. While these Heavenly Kings had to withstand the insurmountable pressure by Long Shiya, his ambush had easily taken effect.

Rays of dark golden light shone in the dark. Under the illumination of the dark golden rays, a shadow with wings spread on its back emerged slowly from the dark.

Without chances of ambush, Zhou Weiqing no longer had any reason to continue hiding and revealed himself.

At this time, his entire body was covered with solid armor, including both his legs, all shielded by the Ringless Ground Purging Set.

Before coming here today, he had already completed the consolidation of all the nine pieces in front of the Ringless Ground Purging Set. Only the last piece was left for the whole set to be complete.

When he released his Ringless Ground Purging Set, the light around his body seemed slightly distorted. Terrifying energy waves flowed through the air, and the only thing he did not have right then was a helmet.

On top of the Dual God Strength Hammers, dark golden light rays shone as illuminating images of crying and smiling faces interchanged. The fascinating thing was that the spot on his chest was shining, and the light rays were brightening and dimming, displaying an image akin to a throbbing heart.

“Who are you?” Meng Gui stared at Zhou Weiqing furiously. He could not see through Zhou Weiqing’s cultivation at all, but judging from the equipment of his legendary armor set, the cultivation of the young-looking Heavenly Jewel Master might not be any weaker than Long Shiya’s! What was more important was that his silent attacks prior to that were just too horrifying. Even the world’s mightiest killers were no match for his abilities to conceal himself.

With a flick of the two hammers in his grasp, each drawing a dazzling triangle, they stacked on each other and released the Six Ultimate Godly Light Formation. At the same time, his ten Heavenly Jewels emerged as well.

Although the Six Ultimate Godly Light Formation was slightly different from the Six Ultimate Heavenly Path Formation, they were similar in most aspects, and it was apparent that his relationship with Long Shiya was not to be taken lightly.

“This is my beloved disciple. What do you think? You’ve never expected this, have you? A Heavenly King-tier powerhouse who had yet to reach the age 23. Is there anyone like him in your Blood Red Hell? Hahahaha!” Long Shiya had not forgotten to show his disciple off at a time like this. The truth was, it was impossible for anyone who happened to have such a great disciple like Zhou Weiqing to not speak proudly of him.

“You’re Zhou Weiqing?” Meng Gui looked at Zhou Weiqing skeptically. He had heard of this name countless times, and the most he had heard of was his performances in the Heavenly Jewel Tournament. At that time, Zhou Weiqing had defeated the Little Demon Shen who had Six Jewels while his cultivation base was merely at the level of Three Jewels. The year before, he had once again participated in the Heavenly Jewel Tournament and won against the brothers, Tian Feng and Tian Ma. Meng Gui may have reached the Heavenly Emperor Tier, but his position was actually below the two brothers. They were the future heirs of the Blood Red Hell.

Meng Gui also knew that the reason Heavenly Bow Empire could stand again was all due to that young man. However, he had never expected that Zhou Weiqing who still bore the cultivation base of Seven Jewels a year ago to now stand before him with the rank of a Heavenly King Tier. His upgrades were just too fearsome. No matter how skilled the Blood Red Hell was at nurturing powerhouses, it was impossible to develop a Heavenly King within such a short duration!

Zhou Weiqing’s gaze was unsteady; he stared at the people coldly. Now, he had already entered another status. The calm and carefree demeanor he bore when he returned with Shangguan Bing’er was now replaced with utter evil. From head to toe, he looked absolutely sinister.

It was no wonder that he turned out like this; these Blood Red Hell powerhouses in front of him intended to mess with his family! When he had first arrived and seen the palace engulfed by the dark Fate Denying Seal, his heart was in so much pain to the extent that he could not breathe.

It was these bastards from the Bai Da and Kalise Empire who had invaded his homeland, forcing his father to resort to such drastic, suicidal measures to protect the last bloodline of the royalties. Facing these opponents, how could he have mercy?

The previous three Blood Red Hell Heavenly Kings had been completely sucked dry by him. He had even devoured the Heavenly God-tier Dreadfiend Sea Naga. It was natural for him to not hold back against these Heavenly Kings. With the Starcrux Saint Nucleus Core in place, his Demonic Devour had escalated to another level. Never mind the Heavenly Kings, even the Heavenly God-tier powerhouses would be affected by Demonic Devour in their battles.

In fact, ever since Zhou Weiqing obtained a complete Saint Nucleus Core, he began to adopt an otherworldly appearance. The gravest mistake of Blood Red Hell had been allowing him to grow. Below those at the Heavenly King Tier, although Zhou Weiqing was absurdly powerful and was much stronger than others of the same rank, he still had certain limitations. However, now that he had broken through Heavenly King Tier and mastered the Heavenly Dao Energy, it would be challenging even for someone like a Heavenly God-tier powerhouse to kill him.

After that brief shock, Meng Gui gradually calmed himself down. He may not know what attacks Zhou Weiqing had used to assault his subordinates, but after seeing that Zhou Weiqing’s cultivation base was nothing more than that of someone at the Heavenly King Tier, he heaved a sigh in relief. If there were to be two Heavenly God-tier powerhouses here, they would be very troubled, but since Zhou Weiqing was just a Heavenly King, things would be much easier.

“So what if both of you possess six attributes? There’s four of us here, and even if they’re not capable of defeating you, they can still hold you back for some time. Be smart and leave immediately. Otherwise, I can destroy the Fate Denying Seal within an instant and by then, not a single person inside will be left alive.”

While Meng Gui was speaking, he had already gathered amongst his three partners. Destruction would always be far easier than construction. He was right, he would only need a little time to launch an attack without minding the price. The Fate Denying Seal below would then be broken, and with a simple technique and he could have everyone inside killed. After all, the people trapped within the Fate Denying Seal for such a long period would be extremely vulnerable. Especially for Zhou Weiqing’s father, the Great Admiral Zhou. It was almost guaranteed that upon the removal of the seal, he would lose his life in an instant as the seal had been sustaining itself by feeding on his life force.

Zhou Weiqing slowly walked forward in the air as if he could land just about anywhere. He looked calm. However, with his every step forward, the grey airflow on him would increase in volume, and the overflowing evil aura frightened even the destruction aura of the Blood Red Hell powerhouses.

‘How is this possible? He’s nothing but a mere Heavenly King, how could his aura be so terrifying?’ these were the thoughts of every Blood Red Hell powerhouse including Meng Gui.

“Master, let me handle these people,” Zhou Weiqing said flatly. Nevertheless, his tone somehow implied that there was no room for argument. At this moment, his emotions had entered a unique state. With only his enemies in his eyes, he needed to vent. Yes, the flames which had burned for long in his heart required a channel to be unleashed.

“Little Fatty, don’t be rash,” Long Shiya ordered with a deep voice.

Zhou Weiqing had his back facing Long Shiya, and said, “Teacher, rest assured that I wasn’t being impulsive. I can’t give them any chances. I won’t allow my family to be harmed in any way; everything lies on you now.”

Long Shiya stared at the broad back of Zhou Weiqing, and thought with a sigh, “This kid… He’s no longer the young man who became my apprentice years ago!” His humorous look could no longer conceal the true composure and strength within. To him, it might be a good thing that he had now truly grown. It seemed that after this, Zhou Weiqing no longer required his own protection. Today onward, he would have a position in the world of Heavenly Jewel Masters on the mainland.

“Alright, be careful then,” Long Shiya said no more. This was the battlefield, and he chose to trust his beloved disciple unconditionally.

Unconditional trust. Indeed, this was the best form of acknowledgment of Zhou Weiqing’s capabilities.

The horrifying energy wave exploded from Zhou Weiqing and in the next instant, the fearsome explosion immediately transformed the heavens and earth to a shade of grey.

Meng Gui was astonished, “This kid actually intends to face the four of us with a single concentrated attack?”

On the other hand, Long Shiya’s six colored light rays radiated once more and showered the palace below in its illumination. Without a doubt, the master and his disciple had divided their roles – Long Shiya would be responsible for protecting the seal from being destroyed, while the four Blood Red Hell powerhouse became the targets of Zhou Weiqing’s onslaught.

The Dual God Strength Hammer in Zhou Weiqing’s hands rose gradually, and above the two hammer were different lights that were radiating from both hammers.

The left hammer was shining in black while the one on the right was a purplish red. Within an instant, two huge silhouettes appeared by his side while he was swinging the hammer. They turned out to be Hell Angel and Demonic Dragon Lady.

‘This is… A Heavenly Skill Projection? But how can he summon two at once, they even look like the main body?’

With just a single action, the abilities displayed by Zhou Weiqing had far surpassed the knowledge of the Blood Red Hell powerhouses before him.

The fearsome dark purple light rays and dark red light rays shone almost at the same time. Demonic Dragon Lady and Hell Angel had been summoned by Zhou Weiqing. The command he had given them was to attack.

Huge dark purple light spheres struck the Blood Red Hell powerhouses one after another, and at the same time, the purplish red light rays on the Demonic Dragon Lady was radiating without stopping as beams of Dragon Silencing Seals shot out without hesitation. She looked just like the Dreadfiend Sea Naga she had encountered previously; her entire body was filled with sharp spikes.

This offensive attacks from the Demonic Dragon Lady was what Zhou Weiqing had learned from his battle with the Dreadfiend Sea Naga. Although he did not have the water element and was unable to copy Dreadfiend Sea Naga’s skill, he was still capable of mimicking its attack nonetheless.

There was no doubt that this attack would consume great amounts of Heavenly Energy, but let’s not forget that he had just devoured the Heavenly Energies of three Heavenly Kings. Furthermore, was Zhou Weiqing still scared of exhausting his energy?

The three Heavenly Kings of the Blood Red Hell seemed to launch their defenses at the same time as several Heavenly Skill Projections appeared from their bodies.

The Dragon Silencing Seal was definitely established, but its prerequisite was that it must land on the target’s body. The three Blood Red Hell Heavenly Kings were no amateurs as well. They possess powerful skills, along with the existence of Destruction Attribute, they blocked Zhou Weiqing’s sudden explosive attack in an instant. After all, Demonic Dragon Lady and Hell Angel were merely summons, and did not truly exist.

The next moment, Meng Gui had merged himself into the darkness. In fact, no one knew that Meng Gui was the true Number One Killer on the mainland, as this title of his had only applied to him fifty years ago. After becoming a Heavenly Emperor, he had retired from the world of assassination, but he was now even more dangerous compared to his earlier years.

Meng Gui did not have Spatial Attribute and did not have skills like Blink, but residing in the darkness, he was not inferior to Zhou Weiqing in terms of concealing their presence.

In the air, Zhou Weiqing suddenly stepped toward the left, with a flash of his shadow, dark Ghostclaws swiped across his initial position, and the air instantly split at that very spot. Even with his legendary armor set and the protection of the Immortal Deity Shield, Zhou Weiqing would be gravely injured if he remained at his previous position.

While he was on the move, the ‘crying-face’ hammer in Zhou Weiqing’s hand smashed toward Meng Gui’s claws almost without hesitation.

Meng Gui did not avoid the incoming hit either. He stretched out his five fingers, and the Ghostclaws bent themselves and clasped onto the ‘crying-face’ hammer.

After all, Meng Gui was a Heavenly Emperor-tier powerhouse – any Heavenly Emperor would have absolute confidence. When Zhou Weiqing had voiced out that he would be facing the Blood Red Hell powerhouses with a single powerful strike, Meng Gui was so infuriated, and thought, ‘What am I in this kid’s eyes? Regardless of how gifted he is, he’s nothing more than a Heavenly King-tier Heavenly Jewel Master. How absurd is it for him to intend on taking down the four of us at the same time?’

Indeed, the Demonic Dragon Lady and Hell Angel who had been summoned by Zhou Weiqing had shocked him, but it did not make Meng Gui feel that he was much stronger than he was. Therefore, he did not show any signs of wanting to avoid a battle and intended to counter Zhou Weiqing with his own skill.

The Consolidated Equipment on Meng Gui was entirely in black, which was barely considered a legendary armor set. The whole set consisted of eight pieces – two of them being the Ghostclaws on his hands. He had always been a Heavenly Jewel Master who focused on the offense.

Amidst the ear-piercing explosion, the Ghostclaws and Zhou Weiqing’s ‘crying-face’ hammer had clashed. The next instant, an unbelievable expression surfaced on Meng Gui’s face.

His body was curled into a ball in the air and flew outward like lightning, similar to how a black cannon would. He had actually been blown away by a single hit of Zhou Weiqing’s hammer.

What was in Zhou Weiqing’s hands? They were the Dual God Strength Hammers! After the legendary armor set had consolidated with the ninth piece, at what level had his powers become? Never mind Meng Gui, even if Long Shiya was donned in the complete set of Ringless Ground Purging Set, he might not even be a match for Zhou Weiqing in terms of brute force. Even the Heavenly God-tier powerhouses would be inferior to Zhou Weiqing in terms of strength!

Now, this was really disadvantageous for Meng Gui. Furthermore, there was still a gush of powerful Devour Energy on Zhou Weiqing’s crying hammer. In just an instant of the previous clash, much of Meng Gui’s Heavenly Energy had been extracted.

Meng Gui had not cried out only because he had been enduring the pain. When he realized something was wrong, he switched from striking to pushing Zhou Weiqing’s powers to fly backward and to minimize the effects on him. However, Zhou Weiqing’s strength was just too shocking; it was likely to weigh hundreds of thousands of pounds. No matter how strong Meng Gui’s cultivation was, even if he had utilized the Darkness Energy in the air to aid him in his defense, the hammer’s strike had still managed to strike a great blow on him.

The Ghostclaw on his right hand was completely crushed and the entire length of this right arm had paid the price to prevent his body from being destroyed by the energy. His right arm exploded and blood spurted everywhere. Only the energies from Zhou Weiqing’s crying hammer had been dispersed.

How was Zhou Weiqing’s strength as simple as a mere Heavenly King Tier?

While Meng Gui was being blown away, there were no changes to Zhou Weiqing’s facial expressions – much as if he had long expected this outcome. With a swing of the two hammers in his hands, a huge grey silhouette gradually emerged behind him.

The three Blood Red Hell Heavenly Kings were indeed powerful. During his fight with Zhou Weiqing and Meng Gui, they had crushed the Hell Angel and forced the Demonic Dragon Lady to retreat. Once the Demonic Dragon Lady was destroyed, they could then join forces with Meng Gui, surround Zhou Weiqing and kill him.

To their dismay, Zhou Weiqing would never leave such an opening to them. When his evil aura exploded, Demonic Dragon Lady’s powers also erupted instantly. Strong purplish red light rays radiated immediately, forming a uniform attack toward the same direction.

Demonic Dragon Lady had actually chosen to self-explode while her aura was the strongest. Self-explosion was a means of offense, and it allowed for her to select which abilities to be upgraded at the same time. Demonic Dragon lady’s mightiest ability was no doubt Dragon Silencing Seal.

After Zhou Weiqing had upgraded his cultivation to Heavenly King Tier, a group attack became possible for Dragon Silencing Seal. At this moment, supported by the Demonic God which Zhou Weiqing had summoned, the combination of Dragon Silencing Seal with the self-explosion of Demonic Dragon Lady had no doubt achieved the strongest level. It resulted in the failure of the three Blood Red Hell Heavenly Kings to avoid the attack of Dragon Silencing Seal even though they possessed great defensive abilities.

Within an instant, the three Heavenly Kings had been left with nothing to defend themselves. Their Consolidated Equipment no longer existed and their Stored Skills had been sealed completely. The sudden changes stunned all three of them.

Just as Demonic Dragon Lady was about to self-explode, Zhou Weiqing had already vanished from the air. Blink.

At the very next moment, the three Blood Red Hell Heavenly Kings felt the space around them collapsing abruptly, while strong beams of Control Skills fell onto them. Without the support of Consolidation and Stored Skills, it would not be easy for them to free themselves from the Control Skill released by Zhou Weiqing with the foundation of Starcrux Saint Nucleus Core, despite their cultivation base.

chapters.title: Chapter 749
chapters.body:
Chapter 749

Meng Gui had recovered his senses from the brief, excruciating pain. After all, he was one of the people at Heavenly Emperor Tier. He applied pressure to his wounds, blocked the flow of blood. Just when he looked toward Zhou Weiqing’s side of the battle again and was about to put his hopes on his three partners, he saw hundreds of sparkling Tricolor Lightning Pearls enveloping the three naked men.

A dark golden shadow appeared before Meng Gui once again. Zhou Weiqing’s gaze had turned extremely cold. At the same time, a deafening sound came from the other side.

Without a scream, the three Heavenly King-tier powerhouses had vanished into thin air.

Upon witnessing the scene, bone-deep chills arose from within Meng Gui’s body. If he had been previously infuriated by Zhou Weiqing’s arrogance, all that was left in him now was fear.

The powers displayed by Zhou Weiqing was seriously too formidable, which had far surpassed his imagination. “Is this really a level reachable a young man in his twenties?” he thought. Right then, Meng Gui only felt that he would rather face the familiar Six Ultimate Heavenly Emperor Long Shiya than the evil young man before him.

“It’s your turn,” Zhou Weiqing spread the wings on his back. The huge grey silhouette blended into his body almost instantly. Then, a gush of evil exploded along the transformation of Zhou Weiqing’s entire being and turned to grey.

Now, Zhou Weiqing looked just like a massive grey whirlpool, sucking away all the Atmospheric Energy from the air so quickly, turning Meng Gui into a hollow being, and sealed every space which presented the possibilities of escape.

Without the support of Atmospheric Energy, even if he was a Heavenly Emperor, it would be impossible for Meng Gui to escape from Zhou Weiqing’s pursuit with mere speed. The only thing he could do right now was to battle Zhou Weiqing to death.

Staring at Zhou Weiqing, Meng Gui’s face had paled entirely. Ever since he had become a Heavenly Emperor, it was his first time being forced into such a drastic situation. Never had he expected for his life to end in the hands of such a young opponent.

Zhou Weiqing may just be a Heavenly King, but the aura he was radiating had completely suppressed Meng Gui’s attribute, resulting in his inability to activate his cultivation. Furthermore, when one had reached the ranks of a Heavenly Emperor, one’s physical body and soul would have long fused into a single entity, making the body a formidable weapon. After Zhou Weiqing had cut off one of Meng Gui’s arm, it was equivalent to destroying his vitality. Meng Gui could not even release seventy percent of his cultivation during his peak. Given the circumstance, it was no longer possible for him to win against Zhou Weiqing.

However, Meng Gui had also demonstrated a mindset of which the Blood Red Hell Heavenly Kings were incapable of. Before Zhou Weiqing released the strong pressure, he had actually calmed down gradually.

“Young man, don’t you go overboard. Indeed, you’ve won today, but if you intend to leave me here, you too must pay a huge price.” Meng Gui said darkly. The stark contrast between his calm demeanor and the insanity arising in his eyes was apparent.

“Oh?” Zhou Weiqing seemed amused, “Well then, I want to know what exactly the price is that I’ll be paying for, to leave you here.” At that point, he did not look like he was in a rush to make his move against Meng Gui.

Meng Gui said coldly, “I’m a Heavenly Emperor-tier powerhouse. Upon reaching the Heavenly Emperor Tier, there will be changes to the characteristics of the Heavenly Nucleus Core. You can ask Big Fatty Long about this. If I were to self-explode my Heavenly Emperor-tier Heavenly Nucleus Core, you and Big Fatty Long might be able to escape alive, but nothing will be left of the city below. Your family and the royalties of the Heavenly Bow Empire, will also vanish into thin air.”

Zhou Weiqing’s expression changed. He retreated backward slightly and looked at his master, who was floating mid-air.

Long Shiya nodded at Zhou Weiqing. Right then, the astonishment in Six Ultimate Heavenly Emperor’s eyes had yet to disappear completely. He had witnessed the entire process of Zhou Weiqing’s battle with the Blood Red Hell powerhouses, and he could not be any more shocked. “Those were all Heavenly King-tier powerhouses who possessed Destruction Attribute!” he thought. Long Shiya thought to himself that even if he were to deal with the combined forces of the three, it would take some time. Furthermore, Heavenly Emperor Meng Gui would be on the sidelines. However, Zhou Weiqing brought three deaths and one casualty with his own powers. While Meng Gui’s injuries could somehow be associated with his own mistakes and carelessness, it was only possible with Zhou Weiqing’s powers.

With the same nine-piece legendary armor set upgrading his powers, Long Shiya asked himself and thought that his abilities now could no longer match those of Zhou Weiqing. Although the powers of his origin were superior to those of Zhou Weiqing by a cultivation base of one Jewel, he was far inferior to Zhou Weiqing in terms of strength. Zhou Weiqing had boosts from Dragon-Tiger Transformation of the Dragon-Tiger Bloodline – this was something that Long Shiya could never match. Furthermore, he possessed Saint Energy as well.

Now that his beloved disciple had become so powerful, Long Shiya could not guarantee if he would be able to emerge victorious against Zhou Weiqing one-on-one. ‘It hasn’t even been a year since we last met! Little Fatty had already grown to such a level. If more time had been given, won’t Xue Ao’tian have to give away his position as The Strongest in the World?’ he thought.

However, it did not seem to be the right time think of such things, as Long Shiya had also heard Heavenly Emperor Meng Gui’s words. Meng Gui was right – when one had reached the level of Heavenly Emperor Tier, there would be changes to the Heavenly Nucleus Core’s characteristics. Once it exploded, he did not dare to face it head on either, especially for the fate of the city below which was greatly valued by Heavenly Bow Empire.

After receiving the Master’s response, the evil aura radiating from his had slightly subsided, and Meng Gui heaved a breath of relief. He could choose to self-detonate. If that was the case, the destruction of the city below would bring about a heavy blow to Heavenly Bow Empire, and similarly, the goal of preventing Heavenly Bow Empire from advancing toward the south could be achieved. However, Meng Gui was also human, a powerhouse who would be willing to risk his life just to accomplish a mission! Having achieved the level of Heavenly Emperor after cultivating for eighty years, the more experienced they were, the greater they valued their lives. The impact of a Heavenly Emperor-tier Heavenly Nucleus Core’s explosion may be powerful, but if the Heavenly Emperor-tier powerhouse were to choose to self-explode, unlike the Heavenly King-tier powerhouses, he would have no chance of survival but instead, he would be annihilated along with his own Heavenly Nucleus Core.

Thus, if it was not the final moments, if there was still a choice, he would never choose to do so.

Zhou Weiqing’s demeanor did not look too good. He sighed softly, “It seems that I have no choice but to let you go today. However, if you’ve been freed, my true powers will then be exposed to Blood Red Hell. It wouldn’t seem ideal for our Heavenly Bow Empire’s future!”

Upon seeing his change in demeanor and having heard his words, Meng Gui was elated and said immediately, “Rest assured, I can swear by my Heavenly Nucleus Core that I’ll absolutely not speak a word about the powers you’ve demonstrated today. I’ll even be willing to dissociate myself from Blood Red Hell, live a secluded life in an uncivilized place, and never interfere with the matters of this mainland.”

Zhou Weiqing pursed his lips and furrowed his eyebrows together. After thinking for a while, he nodded seriously and said, “Alright, go ahead and swear.”

Meng Gui finally let out his breath in relief. He knew that his threats had succeeded, and thought to himself, ‘This kid’s cultivation may be strong, but he’s still naïve in the end!’ He quickly said, “I, Meng Gui, swear by my Heavenly Nucleus Core that I’ll leave this place, cut myself off from Blood Red Hell, return to being an independent cultivator, and never mention a word of what had happened today. Should I act otherwise… what are you doing?”

Just as Meng Gui was about to complete his sentence, all of a sudden, he saw Zhou Weiqing’s chest shining without any source of light. Although there was no energy wave, his gleaming chest was just too strange.

It was a ball of light as big as a fist. Surrounding it were circles of mysterious light ribbons orbiting it like tentacles. Simultaneously, the evil had vanished from Zhou Weiqing – who had been previously showered with evil aura – but now there was another mystical aura on Zhou Weiqing which Meng Gui had never seen before.

His skin was as white as jade, his entire body seemed to have turned transparent, and just like a bright star, he was radiating with brighter and brighter colors.

Zhou Weiqing smiled softly and pointed at the sky, “Look above you.” While speaking, he first lifted his head.

Although he had seen the changes of light on Zhou Weiqing, the pressure which had been suppressing and locking him down had all vanished, resulting in Meng Gui subconsciously following Zhou Weiqing’s gaze and looked toward the sky. It was not a big deal, but at that instant, he was stunned as if his soul had left his body.

In the pitch-black sky, he did not know when it started, but there were tens of thousands of stars shining. He thought, ‘Aren’t these stars gathering into a massive dazzling nebula?’ The shape of the nebula was exactly the same as the light from Zhou Weiqing’s chest.

“You…” just as he had uttered his first word, Meng Gui called upon all his might almost without hesitation, wishing to conceal himself in the darkness.

Unfortunately, it was too late.

With a gesture of Zhou Weiqing’s right hand, illumination appeared as if the galaxy had descended. Meng Gui had no way of avoiding it, as his whole body stiffened, rendering him unable to move.

In his extreme fear, he had an instinct to self-detonate the Heavenly Nucleus Core. He saw the ball of light on Zhou Weiqing’s chest brightening up, and a beam of starlight fell on his own chest, connecting both of their bodies through the beam of light.

His Heavenly Nucleus Core’s movements stopped; even the rhythm of Meng Gui’s heartbeat matched that of Zhou Weiqing.

Zhou Weiqing was still gazing at the sudden beautiful night sky, and said flatly, “Every single Heavenly Nucleus Core is a star, and what I have is the star’s nucleus. In front of the star’s nucleus, how will your star, which has yet to reach its full development, self-explode? I’ll return it to the sky of stars. Farewell, Heavenly Emperor Meng Gui.”
